name: featureboard
description: A local, file-backed feature/bug board for agentic projects. Claude reads and writes your featurelist.md / buglist.md boards directly.
long_description: FeatureBoard turns your project's markdown task files into a first-class board that Claude can manage. It keeps the same `featurelist.md` / `buglist.md` format used by the original FeatureBoard app, with FBF-/FBB- ticket IDs, statuses (Todo / In Progress / Done), due dates, products, labels, linked issues and completion summaries. Because Claude is now the agent, workflow tools like brainstorm (bulk-add) and decompose fit naturally: Claude generates the ideas, the board persists them. Read-only analytics summarize velocity and open/closed counts.

tools.name: list_projects
tools.description: List all boards (projects) under the configured boards folder.

tools.name: create_project
tools.description: Create a new board folder with empty featurelist.md and buglist.md. Returns the derived ticket prefix.

tools.name: list_tasks
tools.description: List features and/or bugs on a board, most-recent first. Filter by type, status, product, label, or search. Returns a compact one-line-per-ticket view by default and is paginated (limit/offset) so large boards don't blow the context budget — set compact:false for full details, and raise limit or page with offset to see more. Use get_metrics for a pure overview.

tools.name: get_task
tools.description: Get the full details of a single task by its ticket ID (e.g. FBF-12).

tools.name: add_feature
tools.description: Add a feature to a board's featurelist.md. Returns the new ticket (FBF-###).

tools.name: log_bug
tools.description: Log a bug to a board's buglist.md. Returns the new ticket (FBB-###).

tools.name: add_features_bulk
tools.description: Add several features at once. Use this after brainstorming: you generate the ideas, this persists them. Returns the created tickets.

tools.name: import_tasks
tools.description: Import a backlog from raw text into a board. Accepts a markdown checklist (- [ ] Title: desc), CSV (with a header row: title, description, product, priority, type, due, labels, status), or a JSON array/object ({features:[…], bugs:[…]} or a flat array). Format is auto-detected. Set dryRun to preview the parsed tasks without writing them.

tools.name: plan_work
tools.description: Turn a user request into board items in one step. Optionally creates the project, then adds the features and bugs you list. Use this as the FIRST step when starting a substantive request, then work the tickets one at a time. Returns all created tickets.

tools.name: next_task
tools.description: Return the next open ticket to work (status Todo or In Progress), so you can pull work one item at a time. Prefers In Progress, then earliest due date, then oldest ticket. Returns null when the board is clear.

tools.name: update_task
tools.description: Update fields on an existing task. Only provided fields change.

tools.name: set_status
tools.description: Move a task between Todo / In Progress / Done. When moving to Done you can also record structured completion metadata (model, tokens, additions, deletions) — these are written to the work log and roll up into velocity/metrics.

tools.name: decompose_feature
tools.description: Replace one feature with a set of linked subtasks. You provide the subtasks; this creates them (each linked to the parent) and deletes the parent. Returns the new tickets.

tools.name: link_tasks
tools.description: Set the linked issue on a task (e.g. link a bug to the feature it affects).

tools.name: add_attachment
tools.description: Attach a file path or URL to a ticket (stored as [Attachments: ...] on the ticket line). Idempotent: attaching the same item twice is a no-op.

tools.name: remove_attachment
tools.description: Detach a previously attached file path or URL from a ticket.

tools.name: delete_task
tools.description: Permanently remove a task from its board.

tools.name: get_metrics
tools.description: Read-only snapshot: feature/bug counts by status, completions by date, and velocity from the work log (tokens, additions/deletions, active days, recent tokens).

tools.name: get_project_config
tools.description: Read a board's settings: products, code location, agent model, prefixes, website, description. Merges MCP-managed config over the legacy project_config.json.

tools.name: set_project_config
tools.description: Update a board's settings (only provided fields change). Writes to the MCP-managed config; never mutates legacy project_config.json.

tools.name: add_product
tools.description: Add a product to a board's product list (used for tagging tickets via [Product: …]).

tools.name: remove_product
tools.description: Remove a product from a board's product list (existing ticket tags are left as-is).

tools.name: get_scratchpad
tools.description: Read a board's freeform scratchpad.md - a per-project notes surface for context, decisions, and reminders that Claude and the board share. Returns the raw markdown.

tools.name: set_scratchpad
tools.description: Overwrite a board's scratchpad.md with new content. Use append_scratchpad to add a note without replacing existing content.

tools.name: append_scratchpad
tools.description: Append a line or block to a board's scratchpad.md, preserving existing notes. Mention a ticket id (e.g. FBF-12) to have it surface in that ticket's work packet.

tools.name: log_work
tools.description: Append a work event to the board's work log: a summary plus optional tokens, additions/deletions, and model, tied to a ticket. Feeds velocity and health.

tools.name: get_work_log
tools.description: Read work-log entries, most-recent first. Optionally filter to one ticket. Returns entries plus a velocity rollup.

tools.name: get_health
tools.description: Composite 0-100 health score with grade and breakdown: bug pressure, feature progress, momentum (recent tokens), and freshness (staleness of open work).

tools.name: get_work_packet
tools.description: Assemble a focused brief for one ticket before you work it: scope, linked-issue details, code location + custom project prompt, scratchpad mentions, the ticket's recent work log, files to read, and a definition of done. Read the files it points to rather than dumping them.

tools.name: suggest_test_stub
tools.description: Generate a boilerplate test file (path + node:test content) for a ticket, derived from its title/description and the board's code location. Agent-native 'fixtest': call it when creating or starting a ticket, then write the returned file. Read-only — it returns the stub, it does not create the file.

tools.name: bug_impact_scan
tools.description: Given a bug (by ticket, or an ad-hoc title/description), rank the existing features most likely affected, by keyword overlap and shared product. Use it when logging a bug to spot regressions and linkage candidates.

tools.name: log_test_run
tools.description: Record a test run's result (passed/failed/skipped, optional suite + ticket + summary) to the board's test_runs.md. You run the tests (e.g. via the shell); this stores the report so the board can surface pass/fail over time.

tools.name: get_test_runs
tools.description: Read recorded test runs (most-recent first) plus a summary: total runs, latest result, and whether the latest is passing.

tools.name: get_regressions
tools.description: Regression view: bugs grouped under the feature they're linked to (sorted by open-bug count), plus unlinked bugs. Surfaces which shipped features are at risk. Link bugs to features (link_tasks) to populate it.

tools.name: license_status
tools.description: Report the current licensing state: usage tier, whether writes are allowed, and (for a commercial trial) time remaining. Call this if a write was blocked, or during onboarding.

prompts.name: project_from_chat
prompts.description: Turn the current conversation into a FeatureBoard project (features + bugs).
prompts.text: Turn our conversation so far into a FeatureBoard project. If a name is given use it, otherwise propose one. Extract features (new work) and bugs (issues raised) and call plan_work once with createProject:true to persist them, then show the created tickets grouped by feature/bug.

prompts.name: process_next
prompts.description: Pull the top ticket off the priority queue and work it end-to-end with the work-packet loop.
prompts.text: Work the FeatureBoard queue using the work-packet loop: call next_task for the top open ticket, set it In Progress, call get_work_packet and read the files it points to, do and verify the work (dispatch substantial tickets to a fresh sub-agent), then set_status Done with a completionSummary and log_work. Repeat until the queue is clear if continuous is 'yes'.

data_dir.title: Boards folder
data_dir.description: Folder that holds your project boards. Each subfolder with a featurelist.md or buglist.md is treated as a board. Existing FeatureBoard 'projectpads' work as-is.
